query: Can you recommend a foundational paper that provides a scalable framework for generating English sentences with controllable semantic and syntactic attributes for the purpose of augmenting datasets in NLP tasks?
criteria: controllable text generation framework scalable English sentences
sentence generation with controllable semantic syntactic attributes
data augmentation NLP controllable generation framework

title.value: Control, Generate, Augment: A Scalable Framework for Multi-Attribute Text Generation

recommendation_reason.value: CGA is able to generate natural English sentences controlling multiple semantic and syntactic attributes

supported_research_question.value: test the potential of this new NLG model in a data augmentation scenario

method.value: combining adversarial learning with a context-aware loss and a cyclical word dropout routine

main_conclusion.value: the sentences generated by our CGA model show significant improvements over a strong baseline, and a classification performance often comparable to adding same amount of additional real data

limitations.value: CGA is able to generate natural English sentences controlling multiple semantic and syntactic attributes by combining adversarial learning with a context-aware loss and a cyclical word dropout routine.

relevance_strength.value: significant improvements over a strong baseline

title.value: The FrameNet Database and Software Tools

recommendation_reason.value: edu/˜framenet) is producing a lexicon of English for both human use and NLP applications, based on the principles of Frame Semantics, in which sentences are described on the basis of predicators which evoke semantic frames and other constituents which express the participants (frame elements) in these frames.

supported_research_question.value: edu/˜framenet) is producing a lexicon of English for both human use and NLP applications, based on the principles of Frame Semantics, in which sentences are described on the basis of predicators which evoke semantic frames and other constituents which express the participants (frame elements) in these frames.

method.value: The project is committed to a descriptive framework based on semantic frames (Fillmore 1985, Fillmore andAtkins 1988) and to documenting its observations on the basis of carefully annotated attestations taken from corpora.

data_or_scenario.value: Our lexicon contains detailed information about the possible syntactic realizations of frame elements, derived from annotated corpus examples.

main_conclusion.value: In the process, we have developed a suite of tools for the definition of semantic frames, for annotating sentences, for searching the results, and for creating a variety of reports.

limitations.value: Our lexicon contains detailed information about the possible syntactic realizations of frame elements, derived from annotated corpus examples.

relevance_strength.value: edu/˜framenet) is producing a lexicon of English for both human use and NLP applications, based on the principles of Frame Semantics, in which sentences are described on the basis of predicators which evoke semantic frames and other constituents which express the participants (frame elements) in these frames.

title.value: Using Structured Content Plans for Fine-grained Syntactic Control in Pretrained Language Model Generation

recommendation_reason.value: We propose to make such fine-grained control possible in pretrained LMs by generating text directly from a semantic representation, Abstract Meaning Representation (AMR), which is augmented at the node level with syntactic control tags.

supported_research_question.value: Large pretrained language models offer powerful generation capabilities, but cannot be reliably controlled at a sub-sentential level.

method.value: generating text directly from a semantic representation, Abstract Meaning Representation (AMR), which is augmented at the node level with syntactic control tags.

data_or_scenario.value: We experiment with English-language generation of three modes of syntax relevant to the framing of a sentence -verb voice, verb tense, and realization of human entities -and demonstrate that they can be reliably controlled, even in settings that diverge drastically from the training distribution.

main_conclusion.value: demonstrate that they can be reliably controlled, even in settings that diverge drastically from the training distribution.

limitations.value: Large pretrained language models offer powerful generation capabilities, but cannot be reliably controlled at a sub-sentential level.

relevance_strength.value: Fine-grained Syntactic Control in Pretrained Language Model Generation
